content.content:
0→# My Claude Code Setup 1→ 2→> **Work in progress.** This is not meant to be a polished guide for everyone. It's mostly a summary of how I've been using Claude Code for academic work — creating lecture slides, writing R scripts, managing Beamer-to-Quarto workflows, and so on. I keep learning new things, and as I do, I keep updating these files. This is just a way for me to share what I've figured out with friends and colleagues. 3→ 4→**Live site:** [psantanna.com/claude-code-my-workflow](https://psantanna.com/claude-code-my-workflow/) 5→**Last Updated:** 2026-02-15 6→ 7→A ready-to-fork starter kit for academics using [Claude Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview) with **LaTeX/Beamer + R + Quarto**. You describe what you want; Claude plans the approach, runs specialized agents, fixes issues, verifies quality, and presents results — like a contractor who handles the entire job. Extracted from a production PhD course (6 lectures, 800+ slides). 8→ 9→--- 10→ 11→## Quick Start (5 minutes) 12→ 13→### 1. Fork & Clone 14→ 15→```bash 16→# Fork this repo on GitHub (click "Fork" on the repo page), then: 17→git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/claude-code-my-workflow.git my-project 18→cd my-project 19→``` 20→ 21→Replace `YOUR_USERNAME` with your GitHub username. 22→ 23→### 2. Start Claude Code and Paste This Prompt 24→ 25→```bash 26→claude 27→``` 28→ 29→**Using VS Code?** Open the Claude Code panel instead. Everything works the same — see the [full guide](https://psantanna.com/claude-code-my-workflow/workflow-guide.html#sec-setup) for details. 30→ 31→Then paste the following, filling in your project details: 32→ 33→> I am starting to work on **[PROJECT NAME]** in this repo. **[Describe your project in 2–3 sentences — what you're building, who it's for, what tools you use.]** 34→> 35→> I want our collaboration to be structured, precise, and rigorous. When creating visuals, everything must be polished and publication-ready. 36→> 37→> I've set up the Claude Code academic workflow (forked from `pedrohcgs/claude-code-my-workflow`). The configuration files are already in this repo. Please read them, understand the workflow, and then **update all configuration files to fit my project** — fill in placeholders in `CLAUDE.md`, adjust rules if needed, and propose any customizations specific to my use case. 38→> 39→> After that, use the plan-first workflow for all non-trivial tasks. Once I approve a plan, switch to contractor mode — coordinate everything autonomously and only come back to me when there's ambiguity or a decision to make. 40→> 41→> Enter plan mode and start by adapting the workflow configuration for this project. 42→ 43→**What this does:** Claude reads all the configuration files, fills in your project name, institution, and preferences, then enters contractor mode — planning, implementing, reviewing, and verifying autonomously. You approve the plan and Claude handles the rest. 44→ 45→**Prefer to configure manually?** See the [full guide](https://psantanna.com/claude-code-my-workflow/workflow-guide.html#sec-setup) for step-by-step manual setup instructions. 46→ 47→--- 48→ 49→## How It Works 50→ 51→### Contractor Mode 52→ 53→You describe a task. For complex or ambiguous requests, Claude first creates a requirements specification with MUST/SHOULD/MAY priorities and clarity status (CLEAR/ASSUMED/BLOCKED). You approve the spec, then Claude plans the approach, implements it, runs specialized review agents, fixes issues, re-verifies, and scores against quality gates — all autonomously. You see a summary when the work meets quality standards. Say "just do it" and it auto-commits too. 54→ 55→### Specialized Agents 56→ 57→Instead of one general-purpose reviewer, 10 focused agents each check one dimension: 58→ 59→- **proofreader** — grammar/typos 60→- **slide-auditor** — visual layout 61→- **pedagogy-reviewer** — teaching quality 62→- **r-reviewer** — R code quality 63→- **domain-reviewer** — field-specific correctness (template — customize for your field) 64→ 65→Each is better at its narrow task than a generalist would be. The `/slide-excellence` skill runs them all in parallel. 66→ 67→### Adversarial QA 68→ 69→Two agents work in opposition: the **critic** reads both Beamer and Quarto and produces harsh findings. The **fixer** implements exactly what the critic found. They loop until the critic says "APPROVED" (or 5 rounds max). This catches errors that single-pass review misses. 70→ 71→### Quality Gates 72→ 73→Every file gets a score (0–100). Scores below threshold block the action: 74→- **80** — commit threshold 75→- **90** — PR threshold 76→- **95** — excellence (aspirational) 77→ 78→### Context Survival 79→ 80→Plans, specifications, and session logs survive auto-compression and session boundaries. The PreCompact hook saves a context snapshot before Claude's auto-compression triggers, ensuring critical decisions are never lost. MEMORY.md accumulates learning across sessions, so patterns discovered in one session inform future work. 81→ 82→--- 83→ 84→## The Guide 85→ 86→For a comprehensive walkthrough, read the **[full guide](https://psantanna.com/claude-code-my-workflow/workflow-guide.html)** (or see the [source](guide/workflow-guide.qmd)). 87→ 88→It covers: 89→1. **Why This Workflow Exists** — the problem and the vision 90→2. **Getting Started** — fork, paste one prompt, and Claude sets up the rest 91→3. **The System in Action** — specialized agents, adversarial QA, quality scoring 92→4. **The Building Blocks** — CLAUDE.md, rules, skills, agents, hooks, memory 93→5. **Workflow Patterns** — lecture creation, translation, replication, multi-agent review, research exploration 94→6. **Customizing for Your Domain** — creating your own reviewers and knowledge bases 95→ 96→--- 97→ 98→## What's Included 99→ 100→
101→10 agents, 21 skills, 17 rules, 7 hooks (click to expand) 102→ 103→### Agents (`.claude/agents/`) 104→ 105→| Agent | What It Does | 106→|-------|-------------| 107→| `proofreader` | Grammar, typos, overflow, consistency review | 108→| `slide-auditor` | Visual layout audit (overflow, font consistency, spacing) | 109→| `pedagogy-reviewer` | 13-pattern pedagogical review (narrative arc, notation density, pacing) | 110→| `r-reviewer` | R code quality, reproducibility, and domain correctness | 111→| `tikz-reviewer` | Merciless TikZ diagram visual critique | 112→| `beamer-translator` | Beamer-to-Quarto translation specialist | 113→| `quarto-critic` | Adversarial QA comparing Quarto against Beamer benchmark | 114→| `quarto-fixer` | Implements fixes from the critic agent | 115→| `verifier` | End-to-end task completion verification | 116→| `domain-reviewer` | **Template** for your field-specific substance reviewer | 117→ 118→### Skills (`.claude/skills/`) 119→ 120→| Skill | What It Does | 121→|-------|-------------| 122→| `/compile-latex` | 3-pass XeLaTeX compilation with bibtex | 123→| `/deploy` | Render Quarto + sync to GitHub Pages | 124→| `/extract-tikz` | TikZ diagrams to PDF to SVG pipeline | 125→| `/proofread` | Launch proofreader on a file | 126→| `/visual-audit` | Launch slide-auditor on a file | 127→| `/pedagogy-review` | Launch pedagogy-reviewer on a file | 128→| `/review-r` | Launch R code reviewer | 129→| `/qa-quarto` | Adversarial critic-fixer loop (max 5 rounds) | 130→| `/slide-excellence` | Combined multi-agent review | 131→| `/translate-to-quarto` | Full 11-phase Beamer-to-Quarto translation | 132→| `/validate-bib` | Cross-reference citations against bibliography | 133→| `/devils-advocate` | Challenge design decisions before committing | 134→| `/create-lecture` | Full lecture creation workflow | 135→| `/commit` | Stage, commit, create PR, and merge to main | 136→| `/lit-review` | Literature search, synthesis, and gap identification | 137→| `/research-ideation` | Generate research questions and empirical strategies | 138→| `/interview-me` | Interactive interview to formalize a research idea | 139→| `/review-paper` | Manuscript review: structure, econometrics, referee objections | 140→| `/data-analysis` | End-to-end R analysis with publication-ready output | 141→| `/learn` | Extract non-obvious discoveries into persistent skills | 142→| `/context-status` | Show session health and context usage | 143→ 144→### Research Workflow 145→ 146→| Feature | What It Does | 147→|---------|-------------| 148→| Exploration folder | Structured `explorations/` sandbox with graduate/archive lifecycle | 149→| Fast-track workflow | 60/100 quality threshold for rapid prototyping | 150→| Simplified orchestrator | implement → verify → score → done (no multi-round reviews) | 151→| Enhanced session logging | Structured tables for changes, decisions, verification | 152→| Merge-only reporting | Quality reports at merge time only | 153→| Math line-length exception | Long lines acceptable for documented formulas | 154→| Workflow quick reference | One-page cheat sheet at `.claude/WORKFLOW_QUICK_REF.md` | 155→ 156→### Rules (`.claude/rules/`) 157→ 158→Rules use path-scoped loading: **always-on** rules load every session (~100 lines total); **path-scoped** rules load only when Claude works on matching files. Claude follows ~150 instructions reliably, so less is more. 159→ 160→**Always-on** (no `paths:` frontmatter — load every session): 161→ 162→| Rule | What It Enforces | 163→|------|-----------------| 164→| `plan-first-workflow` | Plan mode for non-trivial tasks + context preservation | 165→| `orchestrator-protocol` | Contractor mode: implement → verify → review → fix → score | 166→| `session-logging` | Three logging triggers: post-plan, incremental, end-of-session | 167→ 168→**Path-scoped** (load only when working on matching files): 169→ 170→| Rule | Triggers On | What It Enforces | 171→|------|------------|-----------------| 172→| `verification-protocol` | `.tex`, `.qmd`, `docs/` | Task completion checklist | 173→| `single-source-of-truth` | `Figures/`, `.tex`, `.qmd` | No content duplication; Beamer is authoritative | 174→| `quality-gates` | `.tex`, `.qmd`, `*.R` | 80/90/95 scoring + tolerance thresholds | 175→| `r-code-conventions` | `*.R` | R coding standards + math line-length exception | 176→| `tikz-visual-quality` | `.tex` | TikZ diagram visual standards | 177→| `beamer-quarto-sync` | `.tex`, `.qmd` | Auto-sync Beamer edits to Quarto | 178→| `pdf-processing` | `master_supporting_docs/` | Safe large PDF handling | 179→| `proofreading-protocol` | `.tex`, `.qmd`, `quality_reports/` | Propose-first, then apply with approval | 180→| `no-pause-beamer` | `.tex` | No overlay commands in Beamer | 181→| `replication-protocol` | `*.R` | Replicate original results before extending | 182→| `knowledge-base-template` | `.tex`, `.qmd`, `*.R` | Notation/application registry template | 183→| `orchestrator-research` | `*.R`, `explorations/` | Simple orchestrator for research (no multi-round reviews) | 184→| `exploration-folder-protocol` | `explorations/` | Structured sandbox for experimental work | 185→| `exploration-fast-track` | `explorations/` | Lightweight exploration workflow (60/100 threshold) | 186→ 187→### Templates (`templates/`) 188→ 189→| Template | What It Does | 190→|----------|-------------| 191→| `session-log.md` | Structured session logging format | 192→| `quality-report.md` | Merge-time quality report format | 193→| `exploration-readme.md` | Exploration project README template | 194→| `archive-readme.md` | Archive documentation template | 195→| `requirements-spec.md` | MUST/SHOULD/MAY requirements framework with clarity status | 196→| `constitutional-governance.md` | Template for defining non-negotiable principles vs. preferences | 197→| `skill-template.md` | Academic skill creation template with domain-specific examples | 198→ 199→
200→ 201→--- 202→ 203→## Prerequisites 204→ 205→| Tool | Required For | Install | 206→|------|-------------|---------| 207→| [Claude Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview) | Everything | `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` | 208→| XeLaTeX | LaTeX compilation | [TeX Live](https://tug.org/texlive/) or [MacTeX](https://tug.org/mactex/) | 209→| [Quarto](https://quarto.org) | Web slides | [quarto.org/docs/get-started](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/) | 210→| R | Figures & analysis | [r-project.org](https://www.r-project.org/) | 211→| pdf2svg | TikZ to SVG | `brew install pdf2svg` (macOS) | 212→| [gh CLI](https://cli.github.com/) | PR workflow | `brew install gh` (macOS) | 213→ 214→Not all tools are needed — install only what your project uses. Claude Code is the only hard requirement. 215→ 216→--- 217→ 218→## Adapting for Your Field 219→ 220→1. **Fill in the knowledge base** (`.claude/rules/knowledge-base-template.md`) with your notation, applications, and design principles 221→2. **Customize the domain reviewer** (`.claude/agents/domain-reviewer.md`) with review lenses specific to your field 222→3. **Update the color palette** in your Quarto theme SCSS file — change the color variables at the top 223→4. **Add field-specific R pitfalls** to `.claude/rules/r-code-conventions.md` 224→5. **Fill in the lecture mapping** in `.claude/rules/beamer-quarto-sync.md` 225→6. **Customize the workflow quick reference** (`.claude/WORKFLOW_QUICK_REF.md`) with your non-negotiables and preferences 226→7. **Set up the exploration folder** (`explorations/`) for experimental work 227→ 228→--- 229→ 230→## Additional Resources 231→ 232→- [Claude Code Documentation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview) 233→- [Writing a Good CLAUDE.md](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory) — official guidance on project memory 234→ 235→--- 236→ 237→## Origin 238→ 239→This infrastructure was extracted from **Econ 730: Causal Panel Data** at Emory University, developed by Pedro Sant'Anna using Claude Code over 6+ sessions. The course produced 6 complete PhD lecture decks with 800+ slides, interactive Quarto versions with plotly charts, and full R replication packages — all managed through this multi-agent workflow. 240→ 241→--- 242→ 243→## License 244→ 245→MIT License. Use freely for teaching, research, or any academic purpose. 246→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

**Summarizing project architecture and key files**

I'll provide a high-level summary referencing absolute paths within the project root at /Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-aea0751b/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43/, covering important directories like .claude, templates, scripts, and core files such as README and CLAUDE. I'll overview supported languages like LaTeX, R, and Quarto (QMD), build and test commands including xelatex and python quality checks, and mention tools like specialized agents and skills—all without making any modifications.

content.text:
Repository structure (absolute paths under `/Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-aea0751b/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43`) is intentionally split between governance/configuration and a working academic workflow:

- `README.md` (`/Users/my/.claude/.../README.md`) describes the overall workflow, agent/skill catalog, prerequisites (Claude Code CLI, XeLaTeX, Quarto, R, pdf2svg, gh CLI), and links to the full HTML guide (`docs/workflow-guide.html` and `guide/workflow-guide.qmd`), making it the canonical onboarding doc.
- `CLAUDE.md` (`/.../CLAUDE.md`) is the main project memory template: it outlines tolerances (plan-first, quality gates, Beamer = source of truth), folder map (`Slides/`, `Quarto/`, `scripts/`, etc.), standard build commands (XeLaTeX 3-pass with bibtex, `scripts/sync_to_docs.sh` for Quarto deploy, `python scripts/quality_score.py` for scoring), and quick-reference skills.
- Governance artifacts live under `.claude/`: 
  - `rules/` (e.g., `plan-first-workflow.md`, `orchestrator-protocol.md`, `session-logging.md`, `knowledge-base-template.md`, etc.) define when to plan, orchestrate, log, and constrain edits to source materials.
  - `agents/` and `skills/` folders enumerate specialized agents (proofreader, slide-auditor, domain reviewer, etc.) and CLI-style skills (`/compile-latex`, `/deploy`, `/proofread`, `/slide-excellence`, `/translate-to-quarto`, `/data-analysis`, etc.).
  - `hooks/` and supporting docs like `WORKFLOW_QUICK_REF.md` coordinate pre-commit automation and quick references.
- Supporting templates (under `/Users/my/.claude/.../templates/`) such as `requirements-spec.md`, `quality-report.md`, `session-log.md`, and `constitutional-governance.md` standardize plans, specs, and logging expected in `quality_reports/`.
- `scripts/` includes automation utilities (`scripts/sync_to_docs.sh`, `scripts/quality_score.py`, and the `scripts/R/` directory for reproducible analytics).
- `Slides/`, `Quarto/`, `Figures/`, and `master_supporting_docs/` house the LaTeX source decks (Beamer), Quarto equivalents, figure assets, and supporting papers; Quarto uses a theme (`Quarto/emory-clean.scss`) referenced in the guide.
- Quality and logging outputs go to `quality_reports/`, with subdirectories for merge reports, plans, and session logs; the plan-first workflow mandates saving plans (`quality_reports/plans/`) and logs (`quality_reports/session_logs/`) via provided templates.
- The `guide/` directory contains the Quarto guide (`workflow-guide.qmd`) and CSS, which produce the HTML docs (`docs/workflow-guide.html`), illustrating the larger architecture to future Claude Codes.
- Reproducibility emphasis: rules require R/TikZ conventions, replication protocols, and quality gates, ensuring scripts in `scripts/` and source slides stay consistent with the `papers/` (immutable) source.

Key files for future reference:
- `/Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-aea0751b/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43/README.md` (project overview and workflow summary).
- `/Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-aea0751b/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43/CLAUDE.md` (working CLAUDE instructions and commands).
- `/Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-aea0751b/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43/.claude/rules/plan-first-workflow.md` and `/.../orchestrator-protocol.md` for required planning/review behavior.
- `/Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-aea0751b/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43/.claude/skills/` and `/.../agents/` directories for the defined tooling surface.
- `/Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-aea0751b/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43/scripts/` for automation (LaTeX sync, quality scoring).
- `/Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-aea0751b/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43/templates/` for plan/spec/report structures.

This architecture emphasizes governance-heavy, plan-first, multi-agent academic workflow centered on LaTeX/Beamer + Quarto + R, with explicit quality gates and logging paths described in the referenced files.
